title: Tender Roasted Malaysian Chicken Roast
description: This dish features umami flavors and a crisp exterior, making for a comforting Malaysian meal, it is a satvik friendly recipe that uses Shaoxing wine and is cooked in a cataplana vessel.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the chicken thighs with salt, black pepper, and star anise.
In a cataplana vessel, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the vessel and cook until fragrant.
Add the Shaoxing wine and soy sauce to the vessel and bring to a boil.
Place the chicken thighs in the vessel and spoon some of the sauce over them.
Cover the vessel with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Roast the chicken for 30 minutes, then remove the lid and continue roasting for an additional 20 minutes.
Remove the vessel from the oven and garnish the chicken with chopped scallions.
Let the chicken rest for 10 minutes before serving.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
